name: jf1uids

metadata.name: jf1uids
metadata.summary: conservative, differentiable, radial 1d fluid solver with astrophysical modules

documentation:
# jf1uids - Differentiable Conservative Radially Symmetric Fluid Simulations and Stellar Winds

Welcome to the repository of jf1uids, a 1d radial fluid solver written in JAX.

jf1uid is written in a way such that mass and energy are conserved based on the approach of [Crittenden and Balachandar (2018)](https://doi.org/10.1007/s00193-017-0784-y) with a MUSCL-Hancock fluid solver. Note that for any general pupose (non-radial) problem consider using [JAX-Fluids](https://github.com/tumaer/JAXFLUIDS).

The solver's code can be found in the jf1uis folder. You can find a simple example for getting
started under [simple_example.ipynb](simple_example.ipynb).

## Installation
jf1uids can be installed via pip:

```bash
pip install jf1uids
```

For running the notebooks you will also need to install matplotlib (and jupyter).

## Reproducing the results from the paper

All the results from the paper can easily be reproduced via the notebooks provided

- figure 1 → [conservational_properties.ipynb](notebooks/conservational_properties.ipynb): For a radial shock problem, conservation of mass and energy in jf1uids are showcased.
- figure 2 → [gradients_through_stellar_wind.ipynb](notebooks/gradients_through_stellar_wind.ipynb): The gradients of the final fluid state with respect to the wind's velocity are analyzed.
- figure 3 → [wind_parameter_optimization.ipynb](notebooks/wind_parameter_optimization.ipynb): Finding wind parameters from the final fluid state via
gradient-descent is shown.

## Roadmap

- [x] Implement a conservative 1d radial fluid solver with simple to implement yet powerful numerical schemes.
- [x] Implement a simple stellar wind model, first analyses on the gradients of the final fluid state with respect to the wind parameters.
- [ ] Implementation of higher-order reconstruction methods like WENO-Z+, etc. 
- [ ] Implementation of different Riemann solvers
- [ ] Implementation of a shock finder
- [ ] Implementation of a simple cosmic ray model based on the energy dissipation in shocks
- [ ] Two-fluid cosmic-ray model
- [ ] Full-spectrum cosmic ray modeling

variables.name: jf1uids._physics_modules._stellar_wind.stellar_wind_options.MEO
documentation: momentum and energy overwrite

variables.name: jf1uids._physics_modules._stellar_wind.stellar_wind_options.EI
documentation: thermal energy injection

variables.name: jf1uids._physics_modules._stellar_wind.stellar_wind_options.MEI
documentation: momentum and energy injection

functions.name: jf1uids._physics_modules._stellar_wind.stellar_wind._wind_injection
documentation:
Inject stellar wind into the simulation.

Args:
    primitive_state: The primitive state array.
    dt: The time step.
    config: The simulation configuration.
    params: The simulation parameters.
    helper_data: The helper data.

Returns:
    The primitive state array with the stellar wind injected.

functions.name: jf1uids._physics_modules._stellar_wind.stellar_wind._wind_meo
documentation:
Inject stellar wind into the simulation by a mass-and-energy-overwrite scheme (MEO).

Args:
    wind_params: The wind parameters.
    primitive_state: The primitive state array.
    dt: The time step.
    helper_data: The helper data.
    num_ghost_cells: The number of ghost cells.
    num_injection_cells: The number of injection cells.
    gamma: The adiabatic index.

Returns:
    The primitive state array with the stellar wind injected.
here we implement all the injection schemes from
https://arxiv.org/abs/2107.14673

functions.name: jf1uids._physics_modules._stellar_wind.stellar_wind._wind_mei
documentation:
Inject stellar wind into the simulation by a momentum-and-energy-injection scheme (MEI).

Args:
    wind_params: The wind parameters.
    primitive_state: The primitive state array.
    dt: The time step.
    helper_data: The helper data.
    num_ghost_cells: The number of ghost cells.
    num_injection_cells: The number of injection cells.
    gamma: The adiabatic index.
    
Returns:
    The primitive state array with the stellar wind injected.

functions.name: jf1uids._physics_modules._stellar_wind.stellar_wind._wind_ei
documentation:
Inject stellar wind into the simulation by an thermal-energy-injection scheme (EI).

Args:
    wind_params: The wind parameters.
    primitive_state: The primitive state array.
    dt: The time step.
    helper_data: The helper data.
    num_ghost_cells: The number of ghost cells.
    num_injection_cells: The number of injection cells.
    gamma: The adiabatic index.

Returns:
    The primitive state array with the stellar wind injected.
not really ei

functions.name: jf1uids._physics_modules.run_physics_modules._run_physics_modules
documentation:
Run all the physics modules. The physics modules are switched on/off and
configured in the simulation configuration. Parameters for the physics modules
(with respect to which the simulation can be differentiated) are stored in the
simulation parameters.

Args:
    primitive_state: The primitive state array.
    dt: The time step.
    config: The simulation configuration.
    params: The simulation parameters.
    helper_data: The helper data.

Returns:
    The primitive state array with the physics modules applied.

methods.name: jf1uids._physics_modules._stellar_wind.weaver.Weaver.get_inner_shock_radius
documentation: returns the inner shock radius R_1

methods.name: jf1uids._physics_modules._stellar_wind.weaver.Weaver.get_critical_radius

methods.name: jf1uids._physics_modules._stellar_wind.weaver.Weaver.get_pressure_profile

methods.name: jf1uids._physics_modules._stellar_wind.weaver.Weaver.get_velocity_profile
documentation: get velocity profile

methods.name: jf1uids._physics_modules._stellar_wind.weaver.Weaver.get_density_profile
documentation: get density profile
class for the Weaver et al. (1977) stellar wind solution
initiated with the
terminal velocity v_inf
mass loss rate M_dot
background density rho_0
which can be used to calculate the density and velocity at time t.

functions.name: jf1uids._geometry.boundaries._boundary_handler
documentation:
Apply the boundary conditions to the primitive states.

Args:
    primitive_states: The primitive state array.
    left_boundary: The left boundary condition.
    right_boundary: The right boundary condition.

Returns:
    The primitive state array with the boundary conditions applied.

functions.name: jf1uids._geometry.boundaries._open_right_boundary
documentation:
Apply the open boundary condition to the right boundary.

Args:
    primitive_states: The primitive state array.

Returns:
    The primitive state array with the open boundary condition applied.

functions.name: jf1uids._geometry.boundaries._open_left_boundary
documentation:
Apply the open boundary condition to the left boundary.

Args:
    primitive_states: The primitive state array.

Returns:
    The primitive state array with
    the open boundary condition applied.

functions.name: jf1uids._geometry.boundaries._reflective_left_boundary
documentation:
Apply the reflective boundary condition to the left boundary.

Args:
    primitive_states: The primitive state array.

Returns:
    The primitive state array with
    the reflective boundary condition applied.

functions.name: jf1uids._geometry.boundaries._reflective_right_boundary
documentation:
Apply the reflective boundary condition to the right boundary.

Args:
    primitive_states: The primitive state array.

Returns:
    The primitive state array with
    the reflective boundary condition applied.

functions.name: jf1uids._riemann_solver.hll._hll_solver
documentation:
Returns the conservative fluxes.

Args:
    primitives_left: States left of the interfaces.
    primitives_right: States right of the interfaces.
    gamma: The adiabatic index.

Returns:
    The conservative fluxes at the interfaces.

documentation: Return format for the time integration, when snapshots are requested.

documentation: Generate the helper data for the simulation from the configuration.

Helper data like the radii and cell volumes
in the simulation or cooling tables etc.

modules.name: jf1uids
documentation: constants, TODO: bundle

modules.name: jf1uids.units.unit_helpers
documentation: # Unit helpers
The following functions are used to convert from
units of the simulation code to physical units.
When using Paicos this is not necessary.

functions.name: jf1uids._spatial_reconstruction.limiters._minmod
documentation: TODO: rewrite limiters to the one-argument convention

functions.name: jf1uids.fluid_equations.registered_variables.get_registered_variables
documentation:
Get the registered variables for the simulation.

Args:
    config: The simulation configuration.

Returns:
    The registered variables.

classes.name: jf1uids.fluid_equations.registered_variables.RegisteredVariables
documentation:
The registered variables are the variables that are
stored in the state array. The order of the variables
in the state array is important and should be consistent
throughout the code.

functions.name: jf1uids.fluid_equations.euler._euler_flux
documentation:
Compute the Euler fluxes for the given primitive states.

Args:
    primitive_states: The primitive state of the fluid on all cells.
    gamma: The adiabatic index of the fluid.

Returns:
    The Euler fluxes for the given primitive states.

functions.name: jf1uids._spatial_reconstruction.muscl_scheme._calculate_limited_gradients
documentation:
Calculate the limited gradients of the primitive variables.

Args:
    primitive_states: The primitive state array.
    dx: The cell width.
    geometry: The geometry of the domain.
    rv: The volumetric centers of the cells.

Returns:
    The limited gradients of the primitive variables.

functions.name: jf1uids._spatial_reconstruction.muscl_scheme._reconstruct_at_interface
documentation:
Limited linear reconstruction of the primitive variables at the interfaces.

Args:
    primitive_states: The primitive state array.
    dt: The time step.
    dx: The cell width.
    gamma: The adiabatic index.
    geometry: The geometry of the domain.
    first_order_fallback: Fallback to no linear reconstruction, 1st order Godunov scheme.
    helper_data: The helper data.

Returns:
    The primitive variables at both sides of the interfaces.

functions.name: jf1uids._spatial_reconstruction.muscl_scheme._pressure_nozzling_source
documentation:
Pressure nozzling source term as of the geometry of the domain.

Args:
    primitive_states: The primitive state array.
    dx: The cell width.
    r: The geometric centers of the cells.
    rv: The volumetric centers of the cells.
    r_hat_alpha: The r_hat_alpha values.
    geometry: The geometry of the domain.

Returns:
    The pressure nozzling source

functions.name: jf1uids._spatial_reconstruction.muscl_scheme._get_conservative_derivative
documentation:
Time derivative of the conserved variables.

Args:
    conservative_states: The conservative state array.
    dx: The cell width.
    dt: The time step.
    gamma: The adiabatic index.
    config: The simulation configuration.
    helper_data: The helper data.

Returns:
    The time derivative of the conserved variables.

functions.name: jf1uids._spatial_reconstruction.muscl_scheme._evolve_state
documentation:
Evolve the primitive state array.

Args:
    primitive_states: The primitive state array.
    dx: The cell width.
    dt: The time step.
    gamma: The adiabatic index.
    config: The simulation configuration.
    helper_data: The helper data.

Returns:
    The evolved primitive state array.

functions.name: jf1uids.fluid_equations.fluid.construct_primitive_state
documentation:
Stack the primitive variables into the state array.

Args:
    rho: The density of the fluid.
    u: The velocity of the fluid.
    p: The pressure of the fluid.
    registered_variables: The indices of the variables in the state array.
    
Returns:
    The state array.
The default state jf1uids operates on
are the primitive variables rho, u, p.
We might want to add more variables to the
state array, e.g. a tracer for stellar wind
mass or cosmic ray pressure.
======= Create the primitive state ========

functions.name: jf1uids.fluid_equations.fluid.primitive_state_from_conserved
documentation:
Convert the conserved state to the primitive state.

Args:
    conserved_state: The conserved state.
    gamma: The adiabatic index of the fluid.

Returns:
    The primitive state.

functions.name: jf1uids.fluid_equations.fluid.conserved_state_from_primitive
documentation:
Convert the primitive state to the conserved state.

Args:
    primitive_state: The primitive state.
    gamma: The adiabatic index of the fluid.

Returns:
    The conserved state.
======= Create the conserved state ========

functions.name: jf1uids.fluid_equations.fluid.pressure_from_internal_energy
documentation:
Calculate the pressure from the internal energy.

Args:
    e: The internal energy.
    rho: The density.
    gamma: The adiabatic index.
    
Returns:
    The pressure.

functions.name: jf1uids.fluid_equations.fluid.internal_energy_from_energy
documentation:
Calculate the internal energy from the total energy.

Args:
    E: The total energy.
    rho: The density.
    u: The velocity.

Returns:
    The internal energy.

functions.name: jf1uids.fluid_equations.fluid.pressure_from_energy
documentation:
Calculate the pressure from the total energy.

Args:
    E: The total energy.
    rho: The density.
    u: The velocity.
    gamma: The adiabatic index.

Returns:
    The pressure.

functions.name: jf1uids.fluid_equations.fluid.total_energy_from_primitives
documentation:
Calculate the total energy from the primitive variables.

Args:
    rho: The density.
    u: The velocity.
    p: The pressure.
    gamma: The adiabatic index.

Returns:
    The total energy.

functions.name: jf1uids.fluid_equations.fluid.speed_of_sound
documentation:
Calculate the speed of sound.

Args:
    rho: The density.
    p: The pressure.
    gamma: The adiabatic index.

Returns:
    The speed of sound.

functions.name: jf1uids.fluid_equations.fluid.calculate_total_mass
documentation:
Calculate the total mass in the domain.

Args:
    primitive_states: The primitive state array.
    helper_data: The helper data.
    num_ghost_cells: The number of ghost cells.

Returns:
    The total mass.

functions.name: jf1uids.fluid_equations.fluid.calculate_total_energy
documentation:
Calculate the total energy in the domain.

Args:
    primitive_states: The primitive state array.
    helper_data: The helper data.
    gamma: The adiabatic index.
    num_ghost_cells: The number of ghost cells.

Returns:
    The total energy.

classes.name: jf1uids.option_classes.simulation_params.SimulationParams
documentation:
Different from the simulation configuration, the simulation parameters
do not require recompilation when changed. The simulation can be 
differentiated with respect to them.

functions.name: jf1uids.time_stepping.time_integration.time_integration
documentation:
Integrate the fluid equations in time. For the options of
the time integration see the simulation configuration and
the simulation parameters.

Args:
    primitive_state: The primitive state array.
    config: The simulation configuration.
    params: The simulation parameters.
    helper_data: The helper data.

Returns:
    Depending on the configuration (return_snapshots, num_snapshots) either the final state of the fluid
    after the time integration of snapshots of the time evolution.

functions.name: jf1uids.time_stepping.time_integration._time_integration_fixed_steps
documentation:
Fixed time stepping integration of the fluid equations.

Args:
    primitive_state: The primitive state array.
    config: The simulation configuration.
    params: The simulation parameters.
    helper_data: The helper data.

Returns:
    Depending on the configuration (return_snapshots, num_snapshots) either the final state of the fluid
    after the time integration of snapshots of the time evolution

functions.name: jf1uids.time_stepping.time_integration._time_integration_adaptive_steps
documentation:
Adaptive time stepping integration of the fluid equations.

Args:
    primitive_state: The primitive state array.
    config: The simulation configuration.
    params: The simulation parameters.
    helper_data: The helper data.

Returns:
    Depending on the configuration (return_snapshots, num_snapshots) either the final state of the fluid
    after the time integration of snapshots of the time evolution.

functions.name: jf1uids.time_stepping.time_integration._time_integration_adaptive_backwards
documentation:
Adaptive time stepping integration of the fluid equations in backwards mode.

Args:
    primitive_state: The primitive state array.
    config: The simulation configuration.
    params: The simulation parameters.
    helper_data: The helper data.

Returns:
    Depending on the configuration (return_snapshots, num_snapshots) either the final state of the fluid
    after the time integration of snapshots of the time evolution.

functions.name: jf1uids.time_stepping._CFL._cfl_time_step
documentation:
Calculate the time step based on the CFL condition.

Args:
    primitive_states: The primitive state array.
    dx: The cell width.
    dt_max: The maximum time step.
    gamma: The adiabatic index.
    C_CFL: The CFL number.

Returns:
    The time step.

functions.name: jf1uids.time_stepping._CFL._source_term_aware_time_step
documentation:
Calculate the time step based on the CFL condition and the source terms. What timestep
would be chosen if the source terms were added under the current CFL time step?

Args:
    state: The state array.
    config: The configuration.
    params: The parameters.
    helper_data: The helper data.

Returns:
    The time step.

classes.name: jf1uids.option_classes.simulation_config.SimulationConfig
documentation:
Configuration object for the simulation.
The simulation configuration are parameters defining 
the simulation where changes necessitate recompilation.

modules.name: jf1uids.option_classes
documentation: baseline options

functions.name: jf1uids.shock_finder.shock_finder.shock_sensor
documentation: WENO-JS smoothness indicator for shock detection.

functions.name: jf1uids.shock_finder.shock_finder.strongest_shock_radius
documentation: Compute the radius of the strongest shock in the domain.

functions.name: jf1uids.shock_finder.shock_finder.strongest_shock_dissipated_energy_flux
documentation: Compute the dissipated energy flux due to the strongest shock in the domain.
